method_atom: Solving a system of hypergeometric-type linear differential equations (a GKZ system) to obtain the analytically-continued period/charge functions over a multi-parameter deformation (moduli) space, exploiting a duality that maps one description of a geometry onto an equivalent mirror description. Special loci are located where many branches of the discriminant (the degeneration set) coincide; at such a maximally-degenerate point a family of states becomes massless and the full positive-root structure of a simply-laced root system emerges, going beyond the finite set of generators visible in the undeformed/classical description. This realizes a correspondence between the resolution of a finite-group quotient singularity and the representation-theoretic root data attached to it.
object_atom: D-brane states and their central charges on the resolution of the C^2/Z_n orbifold singularity, as it sits inside a Type IIA string compactification on a K3 manifold, which thereby acquires a non-perturbative A_{n-1} gauge symmetry (the McKay correspondence for this singularity).
brief_reason: The GKZ-period solution, mirror duality, discriminant-degeneration analysis, and root-system-from-a-resolved-quotient-singularity machinery are transferable; the D-branes on C^2/Z_n within Type IIA-on-K3 yielding A_{n-1} gauge symmetry are the string-theory-specific object.
